1. Today’s Activities
    1. 1. Download the Exercise Files
    2. 2. Review the Slides
    3. 3. Watch the Lecture Video(s)
    4. 4. Review / Study the Supplemental Materials

Today we’ll expand our knowledge of datatypes by introducing the concept of a sequence of data called a tuple. We’ll also talk a little about libraries of functions written by other people that we can use in our own programs called modules.

Today's Activities

For today, please do the following:

1. Download the Exercise Files

Exercise Files

2. Review the Slides

  1. Sequences of Data and Modules

3. Watch the Lecture Video(s)

Link Title Type Duration
Video 1 Tuples (Concept) pre-recorded 8:21
Video 2 Tuples (IDLE) pre-recorded 11:05
Video 3 Modules (Concept) pre-recorded 6:31
Video 4 Modules (IDLE) pre-recorded 6:01"

4. Review / Study the Supplemental Materials

Note: these readings / videos are suggested for additional context / examples, but not required.

  1. Heinold Ch12: Modules.
    Skim sections 12.1 through 12.4.
  2. Charles Severance Ch 11: Tuples. Python for Everybody
    reading • video
